title: Callothlaspi cariense
intro: Calothamnus cariense, also known as Coochin or Red-flowered Calothamnus, is a shrub native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Myrtaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in wound healing, skin conditions, and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Calothamnus cariense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
